## III. The Gallery — Worldviews on the Spine

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META (v2):** Gallery laid out along the mechanism spine. Kept short — the frame/fact machinery lives in §II. Node IDs (`t-single`, `t-d-multi`, `t-has-rep`, `t-expects`, `t-d-no-expects`, `t-d-no-rep`, `t-d-no-inc`) match the interactive tool. Where two worldviews share a node, the contrast is drawn on an axis or a crux, never on the mechanism they share.
:::

Each worldview is placed by where its catastrophe lives on the spine. Organizing this way buys a comprehensiveness check: if the tree carves reality at its joints, every serious view has to sit somewhere on it — so we can ask of each branch whether it has a champion, read fairly, and notice when one doesn't.

One distinction to carry in. Most worldviews are **located**: they pick a branch — *the danger is here*. A few are **distributional**: they don't pick a branch so much as claim the dangerous branches are nearly empty, and they attach to the branch they most deny. Every entry keeps the same shape — opening scenario, structural logic, who holds it and why, what would change their mind — so they stay comparable down the gallery.
